summary: The plan faithfully targets the diagnosed root cause (agentic tool turns exhausting turn/budget limits) and correctly avoids changing review counts or S/M/L routing, with a documented falsification condition. However, the new success path in external-consultation.sh can silently emit a completed SHIP summary from an empty/malformed payload, and context-only execution makes brief truncation limits a quality-critical edge case that the verification contract does not cover.

findings.title: Malformed success response silently becomes a completed SHIP summary
findings.evidence: In external-consultation.sh run_fable, when stdout is unparseable JSON (outer={}) or an envelope lacking result/message/content, exit 0 passes the cli_failed check, result_obj falls back to outer itself, and build_summary defaults verdict to "SHIP" with zero findings, writing a status=completed summary. dual-review.sh has the same pattern: `value = outer.get("result", ...)` returns {} as a successful consensus payload.
findings.recommendation: Validate the parsed payload against RESPONSE_SCHEMA required keys (verdict, summary, findings, confidence) before writing a completed summary; treat missing/invalid payloads as a failed run with an events record, and add a fake-CLI regression case for exit-0-with-garbage-stdout.

findings.title: Context-only mode makes brief truncation an unmitigated review-quality edge case
findings.evidence: make_brief caps the diff excerpt at 10000 chars and plan artifacts at 5000 chars each (the supplied brief itself shows "[truncated]" in its diff excerpt), and Fable can no longer use Read/Bash to recover omitted context. Neither the summary nor events record that the reviewed material was truncated.
findings.recommendation: Record a truncation flag (e.g., brief_truncated: true with omitted byte counts) in consultation-summary.json and events, and consider raising limits or chunking for large diffs so downstream consumers know the advisory review saw partial evidence.

findings.title: dual-review failure path lacks the structured telemetry the contract requires
findings.evidence: The verification contract requires cause, turn count, and cost to be recorded on error_max_turns/error_max_budget_usd, but dual-review.sh only embeds a truncated stdout+stderr string in the fallback note or SystemExit message; it does not record subtype, num_turns, or total_cost_usd structurally, and it also omits --max-budget-usd from its invocation (only --max-turns 2).
findings.recommendation: Mirror external-consultation's failure event fields (failure_kind, num_turns, total_cost_usd, effective limits) in dual-review's failure handling and pass an explicit budget cap so both entry points truly share one contract.

findings.title: dual-review 'no CLI diagnostic' fallback is unreachable
findings.evidence: `detail = ((proc.stdout or "") + "\n" + (proc.stderr or ""))[-4000:] or "no CLI diagnostic"` always yields a truthy string containing at least "\n", so the fallback text never triggers and empty-output failures produce a blank detail.
findings.recommendation: Strip the concatenated string before applying the `or` fallback.

findings.title: Fix depends on unverified CLI flags; probe is the only guard
findings.evidence: The invocation adds --safe-mode, --tools "", --strict-mcp-config, --no-chrome, --system-prompt, --json-schema, and --max-budget-usd based on the research brief's claims about the current CLI; --no-chrome and --system-prompt are not covered by the stated verification contract, and any unrecognized flag fails every call.
findings.recommendation: Extend review-policy smoke or the real-CLI probe to assert all flags are accepted (e.g., grep `claude --help`), so a CLI version drift is caught before consultations fail.

findings.title: Skipped max-calls fallback records verdict SHIP
findings.evidence: The max_calls_reached path writes a summary with verdict "SHIP" and confidence "low" despite no review occurring (status=skipped, fallback_to_codex_only=true); downstream gates reading only `verdict` could misinterpret this as a passing review. This appears pre-existing rather than introduced by the diff.
findings.recommendation: Consider a neutral verdict (or null) for skipped runs, or verify all consumers check status/fallback fields before trusting verdict.

dual-review 'no CLI diagnostic' fallback is unreachable
Fix depends on unverified CLI flags; probe is the only guard
needs_verification: F1: Validate the parsed payload against RESPONSE_SCHEMA required keys (verdict, summary, findings, confidence) before writing a completed summary; treat missing/invalid payloads as a failed run with an events record, and add a fake-CLI regression case for exit-0-with-garbage-stdout.

F2: Record a truncation flag (e.g., brief_truncated: true with omitted byte counts) in consultation-summary.json and events, and consider raising limits or chunking for large diffs so downstream consumers know the advisory review saw partial evidence.

F3: Mirror external-consultation's failure event fields (failure_kind, num_turns, total_cost_usd, effective limits) in dual-review's failure handling and pass an explicit budget cap so both entry points truly share one contract.
notes: Fable CLI advisory review evidence. Classify each finding with the classify command after reflecting or rejecting it.
